Title: Tomato root specialized metabolites evolved through gene duplication and regulatory divergence within a biosynthetic gene cluster

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase, clade III; Transferase PF02458
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early committed acylation step of root acylsugar pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies first committed intermediate for target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: myo-inositol; acyl-CoA donors with preference for iC7-CoA/nC7-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to myo-inositol to form monoacylinositol, likely initiating root acylglycosylinositol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point/committed-step enzyme supported; required for pathway initiation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly and preferentially expressed in roots/root hairs; negligible in trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seedlings grown under sterile conditions on agar plates; 10-day-old roots analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for accumulation of detectable root acylsugars; knockout abolishes root acylsugars without affecting trichome acylsugars
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlASAT1-L is a root-enriched paralog of trichome SlASAT1 located within the acylsugar biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. CRISPR-Cas9 knockout lines slasat1-l-1 and slasat1-l-2 completely lost detectable root acylsugars. 3. Recombinant SlASAT1-L acylates myo-inositol in vitro and prefers iC7/nC7 acyl donors, supporting a role as the first enzyme in root acylglycosylinositol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA/transcript profiling; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; LC-MS metabolite profiling; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/public transcriptome analysis (274 transcriptomes); coexpression network analysis (WGCNA); metabolomics (LC-MS, LC-MS/MS)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Nested within a previously reported trichome acylsugar BGC; inferred to have arisen by duplication of ASAT1 and then neofunctionalized toward myo-inositol acylation and root-specific expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful handle to manipulate root acylsugar production for studying rhizosphere interactions or engineering belowground specialized metabolism; knockout line enables functional tests

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase, clade III; Transferase PF02458
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective aerial specialized metabolites against pests/pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early committed acylation step of trichome acylsugar pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to sucrose to form monoacylsucrose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key committed-step enzyme for trichome acylsugar pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly enriched in trichomes; negligible in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seedlings grown under sterile conditions; 10-day-old tissues analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for trichome acylsugar accumulation; knockout abolishes trichome acylsugars but not root acylsugars
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlASAT1 is trichome-enriched and distinct from root-enriched SlASAT1-L. 2. CRISPR-Cas9 slasat1-1 eliminates detectable trichome acylsugars. 3. Root acylsugars remain unaffected in slasat1-1, supporting independent root and trichome acylsugar pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; LC-MS metabolite profiling; comparative in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as the ancestral/parallel trichome enzyme against which SlASAT1-L was compared; paper uses it as a direct contrast to demonstrate pathway independence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be targeted to alter aerial acylsugar defense chemistry separately from root acylsugars
